task_statement: CR.v4 P3/P5 customer wording route alignment. Update customer-facing docs so the first-run and launch story names the official Building route as `brick build` with preset_task (`--task`/`--preset`) or graph_packet (`--graph`) input. Do not present run_building_intake, assemble, launch_assembled_building, or goal-approve as separate customer execution routes. Keep helpers described as support/operator or advanced materialization surfaces. Preserve Brick/Agent/Link language and proof-limit honesty. This is documentation/product-surface alignment only; do not create a new route, scheduler, queue, retry runtime, Movement literal, provider-specific law, or source-truth claim.

nodes.work_statement: Update the customer-facing wording in README.md and support/docs/references/{quickstart.md,launch-guide.md,setup.md} so the public first-run route is brick build. Required meaning: official route is one surface, with preset_task via `brick build --task/--preset` and graph_packet via `brick build --graph`; run_building_intake, assemble, launch_assembled_building, and goal-approve are support/operator helpers or advanced/internal paths, not separate customer execution routes. Keep proof limits explicit: docs do not prove provider reliability, customer comprehension, success, quality, source truth, or Movement authority. Preserve Brick/Agent/Link language. Do not edit code or checker files.

Codex documentation/regression QA. Inspect the doc diff and verify the public route wording names `brick build --task/--preset` and `brick build --graph` as official inputs to one route. Verify helper surfaces are not described as separate customer execution routes. Run read/probe checks only, such as `git diff --check` and focused text searches. Do not source-mutate.

Gemini-local axis/evidence QA. Inspect the docs and returned work for Brick/Agent/Link separation. Verify docs do not make support, checker, Slack, helper APIs, or model output into source truth, quality/success judgment, Movement authority, or a third route. Do not source-mutate.

Codex closure. Fan in work, Codex doc QA, and Gemini axis QA. Report narrowly proven doc-route alignment, not_proven limits, and remaining P3/P5 deltas. Do not claim customer-ready, success, quality, source truth, or Movement authority.

not_proven: semantic quality of future graph choices
